title: Public commitment
text: Has the organisation made a public, time-bound commitment to women's workforce participation (e.g., WEPs signature, published target such as % women in active fleet by a stated year), reviewed at board level?

title: Accountable owner and budget
text: Is there a named senior owner (CXO or CXO-1) for women's participation with a ring-fenced annual budget, distinct from CSR spend?

title: Coverage of non-employees
text: Do core policies (POSH, code of conduct, grievance, insurance, safety) explicitly extend to gig partners and third-party dark store staff, not just direct employees?

title: Targeted sourcing
text: Does the organisation run deliberate women-focused sourcing (community organisations, SHGs, skilling partners, women's job platforms, referral bonuses for women referrals) rather than relying on walk-ins and male referral networks?

title: Low-barrier application
text: Can a woman apply and complete onboarding without owning a vehicle, a smartphone with large data plans, or paying upfront onboarding/kit fees, or are financed/subsidised alternatives offered?

title: Women-cohort onboarding
text: Are women onboarded in cohorts or buddied with experienced women workers, rather than as isolated individuals in all-male batches?

title: Pathways to supervision
text: Are there defined, advertised pathways from rider/picker to trainer, shift lead, hub supervisor and city ops, with women's progression tracked against targets?

title: Sanitation
text: Does every dark store and hub have a functional, lockable, lit, regularly cleaned toilet available to women on all shifts (including third-party and gig workers), verified by unannounced inspection?

title: Minimum-density staffing
text: Is there a policy to staff at least two women per shift per site (avoiding the 'only woman on shift' condition), tracked in rostering systems?

title: SOS and escalation
text: Is there an in-app SOS with 24x7 human response, tested response-time SLAs, local escalation (police liaison), and post-incident support, and do women workers know how to use it?

title: Night-work choice architecture
text: Are night slots for women strictly opt-in with differentiated safeguards (safer zone allocation, tracked journeys, night allowances, drop-home or escort provisions) rather than either blanket exclusion or unprotected inclusion?

title: POSH extension to gig workers
text: Has the organisation constituted its ICC and explicitly extended POSH coverage to gig partners and third-party dark store staff, including harassment by customers, security guards, and co-workers?

title: Earnings floor
text: Does the organisation guarantee or track a minimum effective hourly earning (net of costs) at or above state minimum wage for logged-in time, monitored by sex?

title: Pay parity audit
text: Has the organisation analysed per-task and per-hour earnings by sex within the same city/category in the last 12 months, investigated gaps, and corrected structural causes?

title: Human review before deactivation
text: Is no woman worker deactivated without human review, stated reasons, and an appeal channel with a defined SLA?

title: Penalty-free care interruptions
text: Can a worker decline or pause work for care emergencies without acceptance-rate strikes, streak losses, or priority downgrades?

title: Insurance in practice
text: Do all women workers have accident, health and life cover that has been successfully claimed, with claim ratios and turnaround times tracked, rather than cover that exists only on paper?

title: Male ally engagement
text: Are male riders, pickers, supervisors and hub staff engaged through structured gender-sensitisation (not one-off webinars), with behavioural expectations enforced?

title: Certified skilling
text: Does the organisation provide certified, portable skilling (digital literacy, EV handling, warehouse tech, supervisory skills) that raises women's earning power beyond the current role?

title: Disclosure and learning
text: Does the organisation publicly disclose women's participation metrics (BRSR Principle 3/8, sustainability reports) and participate in external benchmarks (Fairwork, WEPs GAT)?

title: Time-bound participation targets
text: Has leadership set public or board-reviewed, site-level targets for women's participation (overall and in night shifts, MHE roles and supervision), with a stated trajectory?

title: Operations ownership
text: Is the site head, not only HR, accountable for women's hiring, retention and progression, reflected in KRAs and site scorecards?

title: Single standard on the floor
text: Do contract and agency women access the same toilets, canteen, transport, creche, grievance channels and training as permanent staff?

title: Women-focused sourcing
text: Does hiring use women-reaching channels, community mobilisers, SHG networks, skilling programme partners (e.g., DDU-GKY, sector skill councils), women's ITIs, referral incentives for women, beyond agency walk-ins?

title: No pink-collar ghetto
text: Are women deployed across inbound, picking, sortation, inventory and QA, not confined to packing/VAS lines, with deployment mix monitored?

title: MHE access
text: Are women trained, licensed and rostered on forklifts, reach trucks and BOPTs, with targets for women MHE operators and no informal 'women don't drive' norms?

title: Supervisory pipeline
text: Is there a defined, resourced pathway from associate to team lead to shift in-charge, with women's representation at each step tracked against targets and structured readiness training (not just informal nomination)?

title: Toilets at scale
text: Do women's toilets meet or exceed Factories Act/NBC ratios at peak-shift female headcount (not average), functional, lockable, lit, with disposal facilities and verified cleaning schedules?

title: Creche and lactation
text: Is the statutory creche (50+ employees) operational, quality-assured, open across shifts worked by women, with a hygienic lactation room, and is utilisation tracked with barriers investigated?

title: Safe commute provision
text: Is company or contracted transport provided for shifts where public transport is inadequate, with routes covering women's residential clusters, GPS tracking, and last-mile door-drop for late hours?

title: Lawful night-shift authorisation
text: Has the site obtained the applicable state permission for women's night work and implemented every notified condition (written consent, transport, security, cohort minimums, welfare facilities)?

title: POSH institutional quality
text: Is the ICC properly constituted (presiding woman officer, external member), trained, covering contract and agency workers explicitly, meeting statutory timelines, and filing annual returns?

title: Equal remuneration compliance
text: Is equal pay for same/similar work verified across sexes and across employment categories doing identical tasks, per the Code on Wages/Equal Remuneration provisions, with gaps analysed and corrected annually?

title: Wage integrity for contract women
text: Are contract women workers' wages paid fully, on time, into their own accounts, with no gendered deductions, recruitment fees or ghost-rolls, verified against contractor records?

title: Maternity Benefit Act delivery
text: Are 26 weeks' paid maternity leave, nursing breaks, work-from-home options where feasible, and no-dismissal protections delivered in practice, including a documented approach for fixed-term and contract women?

title: Social security pass-through
text: Are ESI, PF and gratuity contributions verified for every woman worker including agency workers, with workers able to see their own accounts?

title: Supervisor capability
text: Are all supervisors and team leads trained in managing mixed-gender teams, unconscious bias, and respectful floor conduct, with refreshers and effectiveness measured?

title: Anti-harassment climate
text: Do confidential surveys/FGDs show women feel safe from verbal abuse, unwanted contact and hostile behaviour on the floor, in canteens and in transport?

title: Public disclosure
text: Are women's participation, pay-gap and POSH metrics disclosed in BRSR/sustainability reporting with year-on-year trends?

title: Audit-to-action loop
text: Is there an annual gender review where FGD findings, Part A trends and incident data produce a revised action plan with owners and budgets?

title: Business-case conviction
text: Has the owner/management articulated why hiring women serves the business (labour availability, quality, retention, buyer requirements), a stated intention communicated to supervisors, not a private sentiment?

title: A number and a date
text: Has the enterprise set even a simple target (e.g., '30% women by next Diwali season') and reviewed progress at least quarterly?

title: POSH compliance for size
text: If 10+ workers: is an Internal Committee constituted with the required external member and woman presiding officer, displayed, and trained? If under 10: do workers know the Local Committee route and does the owner know their POSH duties?

title: Beyond-referral sourcing
text: Does the enterprise recruit women through channels beyond male workers' family referrals, local SHGs, anganwadi/ASHA networks, skilling centres, ITIs, community mobilisers, or 'bring a friend' incentives aimed at women?

title: Trial and flexible entry
text: Can women enter through low-risk routes, paid trial days, part-day shifts, seasonal-to-regular conversion, rather than an all-or-nothing full-time offer?

title: Machine access
text: Are women trained and deployed on the enterprise's core machines and higher-value operations, not held permanently at helper/finishing/packing grade while men run machines?

title: Women in charge
text: Has the enterprise promoted women to line-in-charge, quality-in-charge or supervisor roles, and are current promotion candidates identified?

title: Functional separate toilet
text: Is there a separate, functional, lockable, lit women's toilet with water, cleaned daily, the single most cited make-or-break facility in SME FGDs?

title: Menstrual dignity
text: Can women access sanitary products at or near the workplace, dispose of them hygienically, and take short breaks without wage penalty or comment?

title: Fire and emergency
text: Are exits unlocked and unobstructed, extinguishers live, and do women workers know the evacuation routine, with an assembly practice done at least annually?

title: Commute reality
text: Has the enterprise addressed commute barriers proportionately, shift timings aligned to bus/train schedules and daylight in winter, cycle parking, group-travel coordination, or pooled auto/van arrangements for evening dispatch seasons?

title: A route that works
text: Does a woman facing harassment or abuse have a route she trusts, IC where applicable, a named respected person otherwise, and the owner's demonstrated willingness to act even against valued male workers, relatives or customers?

title: Equal pay for equal work
text: Are men and women paid the same rate for the same operation (time-rate and piece-rate both), verified from wage records rather than asserted?

title: Payment into her account
text: Are wages paid on a fixed date into the woman's own bank account (not cash to a male relative), with a payslip or SMS record however simple?

title: Predictable hours
text: Are working hours and weekly offs regular and known in advance, with seasonal surges managed by consent and lawful OT (double rate) rather than compulsion?

title: ESI/PF enrolment
text: Where thresholds apply, are all eligible women enrolled in ESI and PF with contributions current, and where below threshold, are workers helped onto e-Shram and state welfare boards?

title: Supervisor conduct
text: Do supervisors (often the owner's long-tenured men) treat women workers with respect, allocate work fairly, and accept women's authority when women hold in-charge roles?

title: Worker voice
text: Is there any regular mechanism, monthly meeting, suggestion register, informal darbar, where women raise issues and can point to something that changed as a result?

title: Buyer-facing readiness
text: Can the enterprise credibly answer buyer and BRSR value-chain questionnaires on workforce gender data, POSH and wages, turning inclusion into order-winning capability?

title: Annual self-review
text: Does the enterprise re-examine its Part A numbers at least annually, even informally, and set the next year's improvement actions?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Gender in EV strategy
text: Do the organisation's EV business plans, new-plant proposals and expansion cases contain explicit women's-workforce targets and budgets, approved at board level?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Accountable executive
text: Is a named CXO/CXO-1 accountable for women's participation across segments, with KRAs, a budget, and quarterly review?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: EV-skills training with targets
text: Do EV-specific training programmes (battery assembly, BMS, power electronics, charging O&M, EV servicing) carry women's participation targets of at least 30%, with completion and placement tracked?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Inclusive recruitment design
text: Are job descriptions, physical criteria, campus slates and interview panels audited for gender bias, with women on panels and no unjustified requirements (prior automotive experience where trainable, blanket physical bars)?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Promotion equity
text: Are promotion rates by sex tracked at every grade in every segment, with women's progression into line supervision, plant management, network operations and engineering leadership reviewed against targets?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Facilities at target scale
text: Are toilets, changing rooms, restrooms, creches and canteens at every plant and hub sized for the targeted (not current) women's workforce, functional on all shifts, and verified by inspection?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Charging/swap site safety standard
text: Is there a women's-safety design standard for charging and swap sites, lighting, CCTV, visibility from street, sanitation within or near the site, emergency response, applied to both staff and women customers/drivers?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: POSH across the network
text: Is the ICC architecture extended across plants, dealerships, field teams and fleet operations, with channels tested and cases resolved within statutory timelines and zero retaliation verified?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Equal remuneration verified
text: Is equal pay for equal work verified annually across sexes and employment categories in every segment, per the Code on Wages, with published internal results?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Maternity and return
text: Are Maternity Benefit Act entitlements delivered with risk-assessed job modification (especially around battery/chemical work) and a tracked return-to-work programme?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Asset access for women drivers
text: Can women access EV assets without male guarantors, through lease-to-own, subsidised deposits, partnerships with financiers open to thin-file women borrowers, and priority in fleet allocation?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Visible technical role models
text: Are women engineers, line supervisors, technicians and fleet captains visible in the organisation's employer brand, product marketing and industry presence, normalising women in technical EV identity?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Disclosure and benchmarking
text: Are women's participation metrics across the value chain disclosed publicly (BRSR, sustainability reports, investor decks) and benchmarked externally (WEPs GAT or equivalent)?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Women in core engineering
text: Are women present in battery, BMS, power electronics, vehicle integration and software teams at or above 25%, with representation in technical leadership and patent/publication authorship tracked?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Women's share of the new factory wave
text: Do cell/pack/component plants (including PLI-funded capacity) set and meet women's participation targets of at least 30% on the shop floor, with the enabling infrastructure of Domain I in place from commissioning?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Majority-women line capability
text: Has the organisation demonstrated (or planned with dates) women-majority assembly lines, following Indian EV 2W precedents, with productivity and quality data captured?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Women in dealer sales and service
text: Does the dealer/franchise network employ women as sales consultants, service advisors and EV technicians, with network-level targets, training and POSH coverage?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Women in network jobs
text: Are women employed as charge-point technicians, swap-station attendants, network operations and maintenance staff, with targets and the field-safety protocols of Domain I?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Women driver corps at scale
text: Does the fleet operation recruit, license, train and retain women 2W/3W/4W drivers with published targets, including licence acquisition support given women hold under 1% of commercial licences?

module_name: Women in the EV Value Chain
title: Women-owned supplier development
text: Is there a supplier-diversity commitment with counted spend on women-owned enterprises across components, services and logistics, with capacity-building to grow that base?
